item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The second image used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0) as it's owned and copyrighted by EMI Records, Ltd. Thus, it has been removed.

item.title: Re: In My Ears And In my Eyes
item.content:encoded: that website looks amazing! I thought it was real, +1

item.title: Re: Scarlet King?
item.content:encoded: the lyrics have a lot of references to "7 people", which is a recurring theme with the king

item.title: Scarlet King?
item.content:encoded: How exactly does this connect to the Scarlet King? I looked at the page source for hidden text and found nothing. Am I missing something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to like this for the great photshop and altered lyrics alone, but Simon's dialogue needs a lot of work. I can tell you were just working to convey dialect and character, but went a little overboard in the above-mentioned eye dialect and idioms and it ends up a bit stereotypical and ridiculous. I hope that doesn't come across as rude; merely trying to be constructive as I like where you're going with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ A brief response!
- Minimise

"Neutralised" is correct spelling in the UK, where I'm from. I'd assume Researcher Roth is also from the UK. "Lockbox" is also valid spelling, and one in fact used on this site. For a couple of examples see 3714 and 3072.

Listening implies that the people are actively paying attention, whereas hearing is something more passive. In this scenario the people do not have to be actively paying attention, it simply has to register.

This criticism of my wording is ultimately subjective and I used the version I did for the sake of concision. It conveys the same information, but certainly if you felt it wasn't precise enough that's fair.

I decided not to consider them 4150-X instances because the SCP behaves like a contagion; admittedly it's a fine line but it's the difference between the SCP having an effect on people and the SCP turning people into anomalies.

-01 killed himself because after the second interview it became apparent that the interviewer knew about his AWCY connections - prior to this he was pretending to know nothing, and presumably preferred to stay in a cell rather than die. Once it became clear that he knew more than he was letting on, he feared the Foundation would attempt to coerce that information out of him, so he killed himself. If this seems extreme, well, he's an extremist. I think you'd agree he seems highly unstable. Then again, if that didn't click when you read it, that could easily be poor communication on my end.

Other than this, I'll concede that the premise isn't original, but the focus was meant to be on the story created through that. If it landed for you or if it didn't, thank you for reading nonetheless. Basically:

I already changed the date format to be American, dammit, I don't want to change my spelling too!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some Criticisms I have.
Some Criticisms I have.

Here are some problems I found in the article:

-"SCP-4150-1 is to be contained in a soundproof standard holding cell neutralised and buried at Site-19. SCP-4150-2 through 4 are to be kept in individual standard lockboxes."

You spelled neutralized wrong and lock boxes is two words.

-"Beings considered clinically deaf or blind are unaffected."

It would have been better to write this "Individuals considered clinically blind or deaf have appeared to be unaffected by SCP-4150's anomalous effects." (At least in my opinion.)

-"SCP-4150 resembles the 1967 Beatles song “Penny Lane”…"

It may sound redundant, but I feel that this section of the sentence would have been better mentioning that the song itself sounds identical to the real song. Just saying it resembles, to me, implies appearance rather than sound.

-"Individuals who hear SCP-4150 in full feel…"

I think this section of the sentence would have been better with "Individuals who listen to SCP-4150 in its entirety will feel…"

Also you didn't mention that the people who listen to SCP-4150 would become an instance of SCP-4150-X. I would think that since it the SCP is a cognitohazard, that it would make individuals an instance of that SCP.

- At interview log-4150-1.2 "Shortly after the conclusion of this interview, it became apparent that SCP-4150-1 had stolen Researcher Roth’s pen, with which he stabbed himself in the neck. Subject died a week later on 05/02/2018. The phrase “PENNY LANE IS IN MY EARS AND IN MY EYES” was scrawled on the wall of his cell in blood."

Why was the effect of suicide not mention in the description before the interview. If its an anomalous effect, it should be written in description. I know this was meant as the twist of the article, but I think that since we are led to believe the foundation is trying to save lives the article should mention what happens to an individual in the description.

I think that this article OK how it is now, but I feel like it's missing something. I feel like I've heard about a song that causes someone to sing it everywhere and then kill themselves before. I'm gonna remain neutral on this one, especially with all the errors I've shown. If the problems I mention are fixed I might reconsider and give it a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Long crit.
:.|:;

Ok so we've got a song made by AWCY that warns people about a demonic entity or something.

Individuals who hear SCP-4150 in full feel a powerful urge to reproduce SCP-4150 (vocally or otherwise) and pass its effect to as many other individuals as possible.

Is that it? Because later one you say that it also tells people about something bad that's coming. Does nobody infected take a second to sit down and explain "Hey I'm singing this so the world doesn't end". If it is just making people spread it against their will doesn't seem like a warning as much as an annoyance. Up until the interview there's nothing that implies that it's a warning and contradicts it very heavily.

Is SCP-4150-1 lying about it being a message? If you're trying to plant seeds of doubt here it's not working, It's just being confusing.

Penny Lane issn'a threat. It’s a warning. I told ya. There’s things out there you won't never be prepared for. And they’re comin’. Foundation or no.

Again, If this is true why doesn't he tell anybody what this is. Out of universe it just seems like you're making it vague. And that's not good for suspension of disbelief/immersion/etc. Not every villain needs to be sympathetic or have good intentions. Especially not AWCY. AWCY is crazy and has vague goals about chaos, That's it and that's what makes them so scary. Having a disturbing motivation can be much more important than having disturbing things happen.

Which of these is scarier:

  • A man kills his roomate for insurance fraud.
  • A man tries and fails to kill his roommate because he finds dead people without eyes extremely attractive.

Shortly after the conclusion of this interview, it became apparent that SCP-4150-1 had stolen Researcher Roth’s pen, with which he stabbed himself in the neck. Subject died a week later on 05/02/2018. The phrase “PENNY LANE IS IN MY EARS AND IN MY EYES” was scrawled on the wall of his cell in blood.

Okay putting aside how the hell the foundation even let this happen why the hell did this even happen? Nothing he does indicates anything close to self mutilation and writing in blood. Was he trying to be profound? With stuff he already said…? He had a damn pen couldn't he write with that?
This whole section comes out of nowhere and is frankly cheesy as hell. Jamming a guy killing himself and blood in at the end isn't scary it's just kinda gross and confusing. It doesn't work as "shocking" or "edgy" either since it's on the same site that calmly explains forced autocannibalism and fucked up incest cults. Maybe in a void that would be shocking but it isn't on the wiki.

In conclusion this fails because:

  • Like Rioghail mentioned there's very little this does that isn't done elsewhere.
  • A poor understanding of how terrorists think.
  • Inconsistency what it actually does.
  • That ending that just seems to be shock value.

I do like that they seem to have control on things up until the ending though, Having it spread to the interviewer or having the guy suddenly kill him is not nearly as good as just having him kinda sit and say crazy things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Author, (after checking with staff), I added 3 tags to your article: scp, keter, and _image, as well as changing 'acoustic' to 'auditory'. I was asked to tell you to be a bit more thorough with self tagging.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a very good point and I've changed it to make the differences clear. Thanks for reading!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
These are all fair criticisms. I was hesitant to go too far out of the box for a first scip, and in the end I'd have to agree that I was probably pulled in a few directions - which is probably why it reads confused to you.

I did have information on -1's whereabouts, the speakers, etc, but I cut them for the sake of concision. If that made the article weaker as a result, that's a shame. I'll definitely incorporate your critique into any new SCPs I attempt as I become more comfortable with the style.

Thanks for reading anycase.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, the app photoshop ended up being my favourite part of this and I think I'd like to do another webpage-based SCP in future. This is my first SCP, so I think I'll likely get more comfortable with interview dialogue etc as I go along. Maybe at some point I'll revisit this one and alter the phrasing, who knows. Thanks for the upvote, glad you enjoyed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It might be worth marking the bits in the lyrics that differ from the original Penny Lane — I wasn't familiar with the lyrics and had to listen along to look for differences, at least until they got obvious. Maybe bold or underlined text.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry, but this is a solid downvote from me. It just feels stale - the central anomaly is a fairly by-the-numbers memetic hazard, and the addendums (breach log, interview, horror word salad lyrics document, and GoI stinger) are all pretty generic. It's not going beyond the comfortable tropes of SCP format, and it never goes anywhere I'm not expecting.

The article's structure is kind of confused, as if the article is talking in circles around the effect rather than explaining it straightforwardly (particularly in the opening of the description - why not just say it is a cognitohazardous/memetic hazard song, rather than an 'auditory phenomenon…' etc.?), and has weird gaps in it as a result. There's no explanation of -1's whereabouts pre-containment, no seeming interest how the researcher might have been infected, and no info on the speakers. AWCY? also seem shoe-horned in in a way that doesn't really add anything, like they could have been replaced entirely with another group (Fifthists, or some bespoke cult/group) and it wouldn't have changed anything, and the article seems to be bringing them in purely to provide a sense of escalation for the 'sting' at the end of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love love love the GUI for the CoolYet app. I like the gist of all that's going on here. I would have loved to have gotten to you for critique to go over the dialogue in the interviews — the weird eye dialect in the conversation, mixed in with the creepy AWCY propaganda, is a weird juxtaposition between a writing style I hate and one I can get into — but that's not enough to shake off the upvote. I love AWCY? when it's intimidating, vague, and dangerous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Saw this on the critique forum- excellent entry. Fun concept, well written with good execution. +1.

item.title: In My Ears And In my Eyes
item.content:encoded:
Many thanks to Jacob Conwell for crit!

All images are photoshopped by me.
